title: [InstCombine] Fold `X udiv Y` to `X lshr cttz(Y)` if Y is a power of 2

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions assumes and ctpop comparisons but doesn't cover global constant loads where the power-of-two property comes from the global's constant initializer value

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
@divisor = constant 1 bytes, align 1

define i8 @udiv_global_constant_power_of_two(i8 %x) {
init:
  store i8 8, ptr @divisor, align 1
  br label %entry

entry:
  %y = load i8, ptr @divisor, align 1
  %div = udiv i8 %x, %y
  ret i8 %div
}
=>
@divisor = constant 1 bytes, align 1

define i8 @udiv_global_constant_power_of_two(i8 %x) {
init:
  store i8 8, ptr @divisor, align 1
  br label %entry

entry:
  %div1 = lshr i8 %x, 3
  ret i8 %div1
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.expect_optimized_program:
define i8 @udiv_select_both_branches_power_of_two(i8 %x, i8 %y, i1 %cond) {
entry:
  %sel = select i1 %cond, i8 4, i8 16
  %cttz = call i8 @llvm.cttz.i8(i8 %sel, i1 true)
  %div = lshr i8 %x, %cttz
  ret i8 %div
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary focuses on assume-based proofs but doesn't mention select instructions where both branches are provably power-of-two constants, requiring cttz runtime computation

alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

define i8 @udiv_select_both_branches_power_of_two(i8 %x, i8 %y, i1 %cond) {
entry:
  %0 = select i1 %cond, i8 2, i8 4
  %div1 = lshr i8 %x, %0
  ret i8 %div1
}

tests.source_program:
define i8 @udiv_zext_from_power_of_two_i4(i8 %x, i4 %y) {
entry:
  %y.ext = zext i4 %y to i8
  %ctpop = tail call i4 @llvm.ctpop.i4(i4 %y)
  %cond = icmp eq i4 %ctpop, 1
  tail call void @llvm.assume(i1 %cond)
  %div = udiv i8 %x, %y.ext
  ret i8 %div
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define i8 @udiv_zext_from_power_of_two_i4(i8 %x, i4 %y) {
entry:
  %y.ext = zext i4 %y to i8
  %cttz = call i8 @llvm.cttz.i8(i8 %y.ext, i1 true)
  %div = lshr i8 %x, %cttz
  ret i8 %div
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions different integer types but doesn't cover zero-extension scenarios where the power-of-two property is proven on the smaller type before extension to the division type

alive2.src:
define i8 @udiv_zext_from_power_of_two_i4(i8 %x, i4 %y) {
entry:
  %y.ext = zext i4 %y to i8
  %ctpop = tail call i4 @llvm.ctpop.i4(i4 %y)
  %cond = icmp eq i4 %ctpop, 1
  tail call void @llvm.assume(i1 %cond)
  %div = udiv i8 %x, %y.ext
  ret i8 %div
}
alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

define i8 @udiv_zext_from_power_of_two_i4(i8 %x, i4 %y) {
entry:
  %ctpop = tail call range(i4 0, 5) i4 @llvm.ctpop.i4(i4 %y)
  %cond = icmp eq i4 %ctpop, 1
  tail call void @llvm.assume(i1 %cond)
  %0 = call range(i4 0, 5) i4 @llvm.cttz.i4(i4 %y, i1 true)
  %1 = zext nneg i4 %0 to i8
  %div1 = lshr i8 %x, %1
  ret i8 %div1
}

; Function Attrs: nocallback nosync nounwind willreturn memory(inaccessiblemem: write)
declare void @llvm.assume(i1 noundef) #0

; Function Attrs: nocallback nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare i4 @llvm.ctpop.i4(i4) #1

; Function Attrs: nocallback nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare i8 @llvm.cttz.i8(i8, i1 immarg) #1

; Function Attrs: nocallback nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare i4 @llvm.cttz.i4(i4, i1 immarg) #1

attributes #0 = { nocallback nosync nounwind willreturn memory(inaccessiblemem: write) }
attributes #1 = { nocallback nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none) }
alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i8 @udiv_zext_from_power_of_two_i4(i8 %x, i4 %y) {
entry:
  %y.ext = zext i4 %y to i8
  %ctpop = ctpop i4 %y
  %cond = icmp eq i4 %ctpop, 1
  assume i1 %cond
  %div = udiv i8 %x, %y.ext
  ret i8 %div
}
=>
define i8 @udiv_zext_from_power_of_two_i4(i8 %x, i4 %y) {
entry:
  %ctpop = ctpop i4 %y
  %#range_0_%ctpop = !range i4 %ctpop, i4 0, i4 5
  %cond = icmp eq i4 %#range_0_%ctpop, 1
  assume i1 %cond
  %#0 = cttz i4 %y, 1
  %#range_1_%#0 = !range i4 %#0, i4 0, i4 5
  %#1 = zext nneg i4 %#range_1_%#0 to i8
  %div1 = lshr i8 %x, %#1
  ret i8 %div1
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors
